title: org-roam-semantic-sync-all command
description: Interactive command that walks all files returned by org-roam-list-files, computes each file's normalized hash, and queues async embedding generation for any node whose embeddings are missing or whose file hash has changed. Reports aggregate progress. Intended for initial population or recovery after model change.
close_reason: Implemented org-roam-semantic-sync-all: pre-loads embedded-ids, skips unchanged complete files, enqueues missing/changed nodes

title: org-roam-semantic-sync-file command
description: Interactive command that reindexes the current org-roam file: recomputes its content hash, invalidates stale embeddings, discovers all :ID: nodes, builds chunks, and queues them through the async embedding pipeline. Reports progress in the minibuffer (N nodes queued). Works on any org-roam file, not just the current buffer.
close_reason: Implemented org-roam-semantic-sync-file: invalidates hash, discovers nodes, builds/enforces chunks, enqueues async

title: org-roam-semantic-sync-heading command
description: Interactive command that regenerates embeddings for the org-roam node at point. Identifies the nearest enclosing :ID: heading, computes both chunk types, checks size constraints, and submits them synchronously to the embeddings API (not async, since the user explicitly requested this). Updates the DB immediately. Errors displayed in minibuffer.

title: Synchronous query embedding generation
description: Extract the synchronous embedding path into org-roam-semantic--embed-query-sync which calls url-retrieve-synchronously (acceptable for interactive search queries where the user is already waiting for a result). Clearly separate this from the async pipeline used for indexing. Reuse existing org-roam-ai-generate-embedding as the implementation.
close_reason: Added org-roam-semantic--embed-query-sync wrapping org-roam-ai-generate-embedding; clearly named as the synchronous query path separate from async indexing

title: Three public search functions
description: Implement org-roam-semantic-find-best (search both chunk types, deduplicate by node-id keeping best cosine score), org-roam-semantic-find-best-full (full-content chunks only), and org-roam-semantic-find-best-leading (leading-text chunks only). Each accepts query-text, optional limit, and optional cutoff. All three display results in a formatted buffer with clickable org links and return results as data for programmatic use.
design:
Three public function signatures:
  (org-roam-semantic-find-best query-text &optional limit cutoff)
    -- searches both 'leading' and 'full' chunk types; deduplicates by node-id keeping highest cosine score
  (org-roam-semantic-find-best-full query-text &optional limit cutoff)
    -- searches only 'full' chunk type
  (org-roam-semantic-find-best-leading query-text &optional limit cutoff)
    -- searches only 'leading' chunk type
All three:
  - limit defaults to org-roam-semantic-result-limit (or 10)
  - cutoff defaults to org-roam-semantic-similarity-cutoff
  - Return value: list of (node-id similarity file-path heading-text) for programmatic use
  - Side effect: display *Similar Notes* buffer with clickable [[id:...]] links
Internal helper: org-roam-semantic--find-by-chunk-type (query-vec chunk-types limit cutoff)
  loads embeddings from DB filtered by chunk_type, computes cosine similarity in Lisp, sorts, truncates.
close_reason: Implemented in org-roam-vector-search.el as part of yy6 work

title: Load embeddings from DB for search
description: Implement org-roam-semantic--db-get-embeddings which queries the embeddings table and returns a list of (node-id chunk-type embedding-vector) triples. Support filtering by chunk-type ('leading', 'full', or nil for both). Also provide org-roam-semantic--db-get-node-metadata to retrieve file path and heading text for a node-id (by consulting the org-roam nodes table via a separate connection or by scanning the file).
close_reason: Implemented in org-roam-vector-search.el as part of yy6 work

title: Error reporting buffer
description: Implement org-roam-semantic--log-error which appends timestamped error messages to the *org-roam-semantic-errors* buffer (creating it if needed) without switching to it. Used by async callback failures, chunk size warnings, and extension loading failures. Provide org-roam-semantic-show-errors as an interactive command to display the buffer.
close_reason: Implemented in org-roam-vector-search.el as part of yy6 work

title: After-save hook integration
description: Wire up an after-save-hook (buffer-local, set on org-roam-find-file-hook) that triggers hash detection and async embedding regeneration for the saved file. Mirror org-roam's autosync pattern: hook is added per-buffer only for org-roam files, and respects a customizable org-roam-semantic-update-on-save variable (default t). Replaces the existing before-save-hook.

title: Async url-retrieve embedding generation
description: Replace org-roam-ai-generate-embedding's url-retrieve-synchronously with url-retrieve (async). The callback receives the HTTP response, parses the embedding vector, writes it to the embeddings DB, and logs errors to *org-roam-semantic-errors*. A queue (list of pending (node-id chunk-type text) triples) is drained sequentially to avoid hammering the API. Provide a separate synchronous variant used only for search query embedding.
design:
Async drain queue pattern:
- Module-level variable org-roam-semantic--embed-queue: list of (node-id chunk-type text) triples.
- Module-level flag org-roam-semantic--embed-running: non-nil when a request is in-flight.
- org-roam-semantic--embed-enqueue (node-id chunk-type text): appends to queue, calls org-roam-semantic--embed-drain if not running.
- org-roam-semantic--embed-drain: if queue non-empty and not running, pop head, set running t, call url-retrieve with org-roam-semantic--embed-callback as callback.
- org-roam-semantic--embed-callback (status node-id chunk-type): parse HTTP response, extract embedding vector, write to embeddings table via sqlite-execute, set running nil, call drain again to process next item. On error, log to *org-roam-semantic-errors* and still call drain.
- Synchronous variant for queries: org-roam-semantic--embed-query-sync (text) uses url-retrieve-synchronously directly, returns vector or nil.
close_reason: Implemented async queue (--embed-enqueue, --embed-drain, --embed-callback) and db write function (--db-upsert-embedding)

title: Hash change detection and embedding invalidation
description: On file save (or manual sync), compute the normalized content hash and compare it against the stored hash in the file_hashes table. If changed (or absent), DELETE all embeddings rows whose node_id belongs to nodes in that file, then update the file_hashes row. This prepares the file for fresh async regeneration. Implement as a transactional SQLite operation.
close_reason: Implemented org-roam-semantic--invalidate-file-if-changed: hash compare, transactional DELETE + file_hashes upsert

title: File content hash computation
description: Implement a function that reads an org file and computes a SHA256 hash of its content after stripping TODO keywords, tags, property drawers, and timestamps. This normalized hash is used to detect meaningful content changes that require embedding regeneration, ignoring purely organizational changes (tag edits, scheduling).
design:
Normalization rules for hash computation:
Strip from document text before hashing:
  - TODO keywords (e.g. TODO, DONE, NEXT, WAITING) at start of headline text
  - Priority cookies [#A], [#B], [#C]
  - Tag strings :tag1:tag2: at end of headline text
  - Active timestamps <YYYY-MM-DD ...> and inactive timestamps [YYYY-MM-DD ...]
  - Property drawers :PROPERTIES:...:END: blocks entirely
  - SCHEDULED:, DEADLINE:, CLOSED: planning lines
  - #+keyword: lines at file level (title, filetags, etc.) EXCEPT #+title: (keep title text for context)
After stripping, normalize whitespace (collapse runs to single space, trim). Hash with secure-hash 'sha256.
Store result in file_hashes.content_hash. Compare on each save; mismatch triggers full file invalidation.

title: Chunk size enforcement with warnings
description: After a chunk string is built, count its words. If below org-roam-semantic-min-chunk-size, skip it and emit a warning to *org-roam-semantic-errors* naming the node and chunk type. If above org-roam-semantic-max-chunk-size, truncate to that word count and emit a warning. Apply to both chunk types independently.

title: 'Full content' chunk builder
description: Build the 'full content' chunk for a given node: start with the node's 'leading text' chunk then recursively append all descendant headline titles and their body texts. Traversal follows the org-element subtree of the node. Returns a plain string suitable for embedding.
design:
Algorithm for 'full' chunk text for a given node N:
1. Start with the complete 'leading' chunk text for N (same as c2q output).
2. Recursively traverse every descendant headline in N's org-element subtree in document order.
3. For each descendant headline D: append its normalized headline text (stripped of TODO, priority, tags, timestamps) followed by period and space, then append D's own pre-child body text.
4. Do NOT include property drawers, planning lines, timestamps, or keyword lines anywhere in the output.
5. Result is a single concatenated string covering N's full subtree content.
Example: 'Neuroscience. Memory. LTP Mechanisms. [LTP body] Induction Protocols. [Induction body] Measurement Techniques. [Measurement body]'
If N has no children, 'full' chunk == 'leading' chunk.
close_reason: Implemented org-roam-semantic--build-full-chunk using build-leading-chunk as base

title: 'Leading text' chunk builder
description: Build the 'leading text' chunk for a given node: concatenate normalized headline text for each ancestor from the file title down to the current node, then append the node's own body text up to (but not including) its first child headline. Content from ancestor body text is excluded; only their normalized headline titles form the breadcrumb. Returns a plain string suitable for embedding.
design:
Algorithm for 'leading' chunk text for a given node N:
1. Collect the ancestor chain: [file-title, level-1 ancestor, ..., parent, N] using the org-element AST.
2. For each element in that chain, emit its normalized headline text (stripped of TODO, priority, tags, timestamps) followed by a period and space.
3. Append the body text of N that appears BEFORE N's first child headline (if any). Body text means paragraph text, lists, etc. — not property drawers, planning lines, or keywords.
4. Result is a single concatenated string.
Example: document title 'Neuroscience' > heading 'Memory' > heading 'LTP Mechanisms':
  'Neuroscience. Memory. LTP Mechanisms. [body text of LTP Mechanisms before any sub-heading]'
File-level node (preamble :ID:): breadcrumb is just the file title, body is preamble text before first heading.

title: Heading text normalization
description: Implement a function that extracts the clean title text from an org-element headline, stripping TODO keywords, priority cookies, tags, and any active/inactive timestamps. This normalized text is used when building chunk content and when computing file content hashes. Must handle the org-element 'headline' AST node structure correctly.
close_reason: Implemented org-roam-semantic--normalize-headline-text using org-element :title property with timestamp filtering

title: Node discovery via org-element
description: Implement a function that parses an org file using org-element-parse-buffer and walks the tree to collect all headline elements that have an :ID: property (org-roam nodes). Return a structured list including each node's element, position, level, and ancestor chain. File-level nodes (preamble :ID:) are also included.

title: sqlite-vec extension loading with TEXT fallback
description: On DB open, attempt to load the sqlite-vec extension from a customizable org-roam-semantic-sqlite-vec-path. If loading succeeds, store embeddings as sqlite-vec blobs (vec_f32 format). If extension is unavailable, fall back to storing vectors as space-separated TEXT. Record which format is in use so read/write code uses the correct serialization. Always perform cosine similarity in Emacs Lisp regardless of storage format.
close_reason: Implemented in org-roam-vector-search.el as part of yy6 work

title: DB schema design and connection management
description: Define the embeddings DB schema: a 'file_hashes' table (file TEXT PK, content_hash TEXT, updated_at INTEGER) and an 'embeddings' table (node_id TEXT, chunk_type TEXT, embedding BLOB/TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (node_id, chunk_type)). Implement open/close singleton connection using Emacs 29 built-in sqlite-open, with a customizable org-roam-semantic-db-location variable defaulting to a sibling of org-roam-db-location.
design:
Schema DDL:
  CREATE TABLE file_hashes (
    file TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL
  );
  CREATE TABLE embeddings (
    node_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    chunk_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    embedding BLOB NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (node_id, chunk_type)
  );
chunk_type values are the strings 'leading' and 'full'.
embedding column stores sqlite-vec float32 blob when extension is loaded, else space-separated floats as TEXT cast to BLOB.
Connection singleton: a module-level variable org-roam-semantic--db-connection holds the open connection. org-roam-semantic--db-open returns it (opening if nil), org-roam-semantic--db-close sets it to nil. Use sqlite-open / sqlite-execute / sqlite-select (Emacs 29 built-in, NOT emacsql).
Location variable: org-roam-semantic-db-location defaults to (file-name-concat (file-name-directory org-roam-db-location) "org-roam-embeddings.db").
close_reason: Implemented in org-roam-vector-search.el as part of yy6 work

title: Manual sync commands
description: Provide interactive commands for users to trigger embedding (re)generation on demand at three scopes: current heading, current file, and all org-roam files. These commands identify stale or missing embeddings and drive the same async generation pipeline used by the save hook.
close_reason: All constituent tasks implemented; work complete

title: Search and retrieval from embeddings DB
description: Rewrite the similarity search layer to load embeddings from the SQLite DB rather than scanning org files. Provide three public search functions: org-roam-semantic-find-best (searches both chunk types, deduplicates by node, keeps best score), org-roam-semantic-find-best-full (full-content chunks only), and org-roam-semantic-find-best-leading (leading-text chunks only). Query embedding generation remains synchronous.
close_reason: All constituent tasks implemented; work complete

title: Async embedding generation pipeline
description: Replace synchronous url-retrieve-synchronously calls with async url-retrieve so that save hooks never block Emacs. The pipeline hashes file content (normalized: no TODO/tags/properties/timestamps) to detect changes, invalidates all stored embeddings for that file on change, then queues async API calls per chunk. Errors are routed to an *org-roam-semantic-errors* buffer.
close_reason: All constituent tasks implemented; work complete

title: org-element chunking engine
description: Replace the current regex-based heading scanner with an org-element tree traversal that discovers only org-roam nodes (:ID: headings) and builds two chunk types per node: 'leading text' (ancestor breadcrumb + local pre-subsection body) and 'full content' (leading text + all recursive sub-content). Heading text is normalized by stripping TODO markers, tags, timestamps, and properties.
close_reason: All constituent tasks implemented; work complete

title: Separate SQLite embeddings database
description: Establish a dedicated org-roam-embeddings.db SQLite file alongside org-roam.db to store vector embeddings, replacing the current EMBEDDING org property storage. This is the foundational infrastructure that all other epics depend on.
close_reason: All constituent tasks implemented; work complete

title: Replace section finding by heading name with org-element-map in org-roam-vector-search.el
description: Lines ~591 and ~631 use (re-search-forward (format "^\\*+\\s-+%s\\s-*$" (regexp-quote identifier)) nil t) to locate a heading by its exact text. Replace with org-element-map on 'headline filtering by :raw-value equality, returning the element whose :raw-value matches the identifier. Then use (goto-char (org-element-property :begin element)) to position point.
design: org-element-map traverses the whole tree; for large files a recursive-edit-stopping traversal may be needed for efficiency. The :raw-value of a headline already has TODO keywords, priority cookies, and tags stripped in org-element 29+, so the match is against the clean title. Affected: org-roam-semantic--find-or-create-section and the section-content-end helper.

title: Replace properties drawer navigation with org-entry-get in org-roam-vector-search.el
description: Lines ~195, 694-700 use re-search-forward "^:END:" and looking-at "^:PROPERTIES:" to skip past property drawers and extract :ID: values. Line ~1432 uses re-search-forward "^:ID:\\s-*\\(.+\\)$" to retrieve the file-level node ID. Replace with org-entry-get (for single property lookup) or org-element-at-point on the property-drawer element (for bulk reads). Use org-id-get for the :ID: property specifically.
design: org-id-get returns the :ID: property of the heading at point, or nil. For skipping past property drawers during traversal, org-element-map on headline already gives :contents-begin which is post-property-drawer -- no need to manually skip. Affected: org-roam-semantic--build-node-chunk body extractor, org-roam-semantic--extract-node-embedding, org-roam-semantic--extract-file-nodes.

title: Replace heading traversal in chunking with org-element-map on headline in org-roam-vector-search.el
description: The main chunking loop (~line 421) uses (re-search-forward "^\\*+ " nil t) then checks org-at-heading-p. The body-extraction block (~lines 472-479) uses looking-at patterns to read heading level (number of asterisks), raw text, and content end boundary. Replace with org-element-map on the parsed buffer using the headline type. org-element headings carry :level, :raw-value, :begin, :end, :contents-begin, :contents-end directly.
design: Parse once with (org-element-parse-buffer) then traverse with org-element-map. Use :raw-value for heading text (gives pre-normalized title). Use :contents-begin and the :begin of the next same-or-higher-level sibling for content boundaries -- org-element-property :end gives the full subtree end. Avoids the re-search trick for finding the next sibling boundary.

title: Replace #+title: extraction with org-collect-keywords in org-roam-vector-search.el
description: Four separate functions call (re-search-forward "^#\\+title:\\s-*\\(.+\\)$" nil t) to get the file title: the file-level chunk builder, the heading chunk builder, the change-detection pre-hash function, and the node-ID extractor. Replace with (org-collect-keywords '("TITLE")) or equivalent org-element-keyword traversal, which is the idiomatic API for reading document keywords.
design: (org-collect-keywords '("TITLE")) returns an alist like (("TITLE" "My Note Title")). For the change-detection path the buffer is a temp copy, so org-element still applies. Affected functions: org-roam-semantic--build-file-chunk, org-roam-semantic--build-node-chunk, org-roam-semantic--normalize-content (only the title-read, NOT the normalization regexes), org-roam-semantic--extract-file-nodes.

title: Migrate re-search-forward to org-element in org-roam-vector-search.el
description: The package core file uses ~50 re-search-forward / looking-at calls to parse org structure (titles, headings, property drawers, section boundaries). Replacing with org-element API makes the code more robust and more readable. NOTE: The content normalization block in org-roam-semantic--normalize-content (~lines 1211-1245) and EMBEDDING property cleanup loops are intentionally regex-based -- they perform batch text mutations on a temp buffer and are correctly expressed as regexes; do not replace those. All other search calls are candidates.
design: Replace by pattern type: (1) org-collect-keywords for #+title: reads, (2) org-element-map on headline type for heading traversal, (3) org-entry-get / org-element-property for property lookups, (4) org-element-map filter by :raw-value for section finding by name. The blank-line navigation re-search-forward calls are movement-only and have no clean org-element equivalent -- leave as-is.

title: org-roam-semantic-clean-properties command
description: Interactive command that scans all org-roam files and removes all :EMBEDDING: properties from headings and file-level drawers. Uses org-element to locate and delete the properties without disturbing other content. Prompts for confirmation before modifying files. Reports count of files and properties cleaned.

title: EMBEDDING property handling config variable
description: Add org-roam-semantic-clean-old-properties (default nil) controlling whether legacy EMBEDDING properties are stripped from org files during sync. When nil, old properties are silently ignored. When t, any EMBEDDING property encountered during file processing is removed from the file. This is a user-configurable transition aid.

title: Migration and backward compatibility
description: Handle the transition from property-based EMBEDDING storage to the new DB. Provide a configurable variable governing whether old EMBEDDING properties are ignored (default) or deleted when encountered. Provide an interactive command to strip EMBEDDING properties from all org-roam files.
close_reason: All constituent tasks implemented; work complete

title: Replace title extraction and heading search with org-element in org-agenda-project-tracking.el
description: Two re-search-forward calls in this file: (1) my/org-roam-log-state-change-to-daily uses re-search-forward "^#\\+title: \\(.+\\)$" to get the project title from file keywords. (2) my/org-roam-change-task-state uses re-search-forward with a dynamic pattern combining a TODO keyword set and a heading text to find and change a task state. Replace (1) with org-collect-keywords and (2) with org-element-map on 'headline filtering by both :todo-keyword and :raw-value.
design: For (2): (org-element-map (org-element-parse-buffer) 'headline (lambda (h) (when (and (member (org-element-property :todo-keyword h) '("TODO" "IN-PROGRESS" "BLOCKED" "DONE" "CANCELLED")) (string= (org-element-property :raw-value h) heading)) h)) nil t) returns the matching element; then go to :begin and call org-todo.

title: Replace keyword/filetags metadata patterns with org-collect-keywords in org-roam-api.el
description: Multiple functions search for #+title: and #+filetags: lines (~lines 1458, 1464, 1652, 1692-1707) using re-search-forward. Reads should use org-collect-keywords ('("TITLE" "FILETAGS")). Writes (inserting or replacing #+filetags:) should use org-element-map on 'keyword to locate the element then do targeted buffer edits, replacing the current regex replace-match approach.
design: org-collect-keywords returns the first matching keyword by default. For #+filetags: the value includes the colon-delimited tags string. For inserting a new #+filetags: line when none exists, find the #+title: keyword element via org-element-map and insert after its :end position. This is more robust than searching for #+title:.*$ as an anchor.

title: Replace draft example-block parsing with org-element in org-roam-api.el
description: my/api-get-draft and my/api-update-draft use search-forward literals to navigate: search-forward "* Current Draft" finds a heading, then search-forward "#+begin_example" / "#+end_example" bound a content block (~lines 416-420, 481-485, 516-520). Replace with org-element: find the Current Draft headline with org-element-map on 'headline, then find the 'example-block child element within it. Read or replace the :value of the example-block element rather than positional buffer surgery.
design: An example-block element has :value containing the block text (without the #+begin_example/#+end_example delimiters). For updates, use (delete-region begin end) + insert on the element's :begin/:end. The example-block is a child of the Current Draft section's content -- traverse with org-element-map restricted to that subtree.

title: Replace section and inbox navigation with org-element-map on headline in org-roam-api.el
description: Several functions navigate to sections by heading name: inbox section find (~lines 692, 1177, 1317) uses re-search-forward "^\\* Inbox$" then loops with looking-at patterns to scan entries; section-content operations (~line 978) use re-search-forward with a format string; heading search (~line 1738) uses re-search-forward for any-level heading by name. The inbox-history parser (~lines 1181-1192) also reads structured heading text. Replace heading finds with org-element-map on 'headline filtered by :raw-value; replace inline loop looking-at conditions with element boundary arithmetic.
design: For inbox history entries, each entry is a sub-headline with :todo-keyword and :raw-value. Replace looking-at "^\\*\\* \\(DONE\\|TODO\\) /\\([^ ]+\\) -> \\(.+\\)$" with org-element-property access: :todo-keyword for DONE/TODO, :raw-value for the rest. This avoids fragile line-by-line parsing of structured headings.

title: Replace property extraction in my/api--extract-properties with org-entry-get
description: my/api--extract-properties is a duplicate of the sb/--extract-properties pattern in org-roam-second-brain.el: it manually loops through a PROPERTIES drawer using re-search-forward "^:PROPERTIES:", forward-line, and looking-at to build an alist. Replace with (org-entry-properties nil 'standard) which returns the same structure without hand-rolled parsing. Also affects the individual :ORIGINAL-REQUEST:, :CREATED:, :LAST-UPDATED:, :CAPTURED:, :ORIGINAL: property reads (~lines 408, 411, 475, 1190, 1192) -- replace with org-entry-get for each.
design: org-entry-get takes the heading position and property name (without colons). For inline scattered reads like (re-search-forward ":CREATED: \\(.+\\)" nil t), replace with (org-entry-get (point) "CREATED") after positioning point at the heading. Confirm that all these properties are in a PROPERTIES drawer (not just text lines) before replacing.

title: Replace blog keyword/metadata extraction with org-collect-keywords in org-roam-second-brain.el
description: Four functions use re-search-forward to read and write org file keywords: sb/--blog-is-draft reads #+hugo_draft:, sb/--blog-set-draft-value reads and replaces #+hugo_draft: / #+date:, sb/--blog-get-property reads drawer :PROPERTY: values, sb/blog-generate-outline reads #+title:. Replace reads with org-collect-keywords ('("HUGO_DRAFT" "TITLE" "DATE")). For writes, use org-roam or org-element based keyword replacement rather than regex replace-match.
design: org-collect-keywords returns an alist of keyword-name -> value. For setting a keyword, the pattern is: find the keyword line with org-element-map on 'keyword filtered by :key, then replace the buffer substring at the element's :begin/:end. Alternatively, use (org-set-property) for properties or a helper that works with the element tree. Note sb/--blog-get-property also reads PROPERTIES drawer entries -- keep that part with org-entry-get.

title: Replace checkbox item scanning with org-element-map on item in org-roam-second-brain.el
description: Two functions scan for unchecked checkbox items: sb/core-pending-followups (~line 425 and 444) and sb/core-dangling-person-links (~line 465). Both loop with (while (re-search-forward "^[ \t]*- \\[ \\] \\(.*\\)$" nil t)) collecting matching items. Replace with org-element-map on 'item filtering by (:checkbox . off), then extract the item text from the element's content.
design: org-element item elements have :checkbox property ('on, 'off, 'trans, or nil). Filter for 'off to get unchecked items. The paragraph text inside the item is accessible via the item's children. For the dangling-links case, also inspect the paragraph content for org-link elements (org-element-map on 'link with :type "id" within each item).

title: Replace section navigation with org-element-map on headline in org-roam-second-brain.el
description: Multiple functions find sections by heading name and walk within section boundaries using re-search-forward: sb/--extract-unchecked-items (finds section, loops until next heading), sb/--daily-has-related-section-p, sb/--daily-remove-related-section (finds and deletes section), sb/inbox (finds Inbox heading to append), sb/--blog-has-content (finds Draft section and its end), sb/--blog-outline-progress (finds Outline section), sb/blog-from-idea (re-search-backward for Research), sb/blog-generate-outline, sb/blog-expand-section. Replace with org-element-map on 'headline filtered by :raw-value, then use :begin/:end/:contents-begin/:contents-end for bounds.
design: Common pattern: find element with (org-element-map (org-element-parse-buffer) 'headline (lambda (h) (when (string= (org-element-property :raw-value h) name) h)) nil t). Use :begin to go to heading, :contents-begin and :end to bound content. For the daily-remove-related-section case, use delete-region on the element bounds. Note: org-element-parse-buffer is expensive on large files -- consider org-element-at-point with re-scan if performance matters.

title: Replace property extraction in sb/--extract-properties with org-entry-get
description: sb/--extract-properties manually loops through a PROPERTIES drawer using re-search-forward "^:PROPERTIES:", forward-line, and looking-at patterns to build a key-value alist. This is fragile hand-rolled parsing of a structure org already understands. Replace with (org-entry-properties nil 'standard) which returns an alist of all standard properties at point, or use individual org-entry-get calls for specific keys.
design: org-entry-properties returns a list of (NAME . VALUE) pairs for all :KEY: entries in the nearest enclosing property drawer. For file-level callers (point-min), use org-collect-keywords for #+KEY: lines and org-entry-properties for :KEY: drawer entries. The function is called in multiple places so the replacement should be a drop-in that returns the same alist structure.

title: Migrate re-search-forward to org-element in org-agenda-project-tracking.el
description: Two re-search-forward calls only: (1) #+title: extraction in my/org-roam-log-state-change-to-daily, (2) a heading search for a specific task+TODO-keyword combo in my/org-roam-change-task-state. Small file; single task covers both changes.
design: Replace #+title: read with org-collect-keywords. Replace heading+TODO search with org-element-map on headline type filtering by :todo-keyword and :raw-value (org-element stores the TODO state separately from the title text).

title: Migrate re-search-forward to org-element in org-roam-api.el
description: ~40 re-search-forward / looking-at / search-forward calls span property extraction, section/inbox navigation, draft example-block parsing, and keyword/filetags metadata. Replacing with org-element API improves correctness and maintainability. Organized by pattern type.
design: Property extraction -> org-entry-get or org-entry-properties. Section and inbox navigation -> org-element-map on headline type. Draft example blocks -> org-element-map on example-block type. Keyword/filetags (#+title:, #+filetags:) -> org-collect-keywords for reads; org-element-based replacement for writes.

title: Migrate re-search-forward to org-element in org-roam-second-brain.el
description: ~30 re-search-forward / looking-at calls handle property extraction, section navigation, checkbox scanning, and keyword/blog-metadata access. Replacing with org-element API improves correctness and maintainability. Organized by pattern type rather than by individual function.
design: Property extraction -> org-entry-get or org-entry-properties. Section navigation -> org-element-map on headline type. Checkbox scanning -> org-element-map on item type filtering by :checkbox state. Blog keyword metadata (#+hugo_draft:, #+title:, #+date:) -> org-collect-keywords for reads; org-element-based replacement for writes.

value: architecture: async pipeline. Embedding generation for indexing is ASYNC via url-retrieve with callback. A module-level queue (list of (node-id chunk-type text) triples) and a running flag prevent concurrent requests and serialize API calls. Callback writes vector to embeddings DB then drains next queue item. Embedding generation for search QUERIES is SYNCHRONOUS (url-retrieve-synchronously) — user is already waiting. All async errors go to *org-roam-semantic-errors* buffer.

architecture: chunking. Only embed org-roam nodes (headings with :ID: property). Two chunk types per node: 'leading' and 'full'. Heading text normalization: strip TODO keywords, priority cookies [#X], tags :tag:, and timestamps from org-element headline — keep only plain title text. 'leading' chunk = normalized ancestor headline titles (breadcrumb from file title to node) + node's own pre-child body text. 'full' chunk = leading chunk + all descendant normalized headline titles and their pre-child body texts, recursively. Property drawers, planning lines, keywords never included in chunk text. Min size: skip + warn to *org-roam-semantic-errors*. Max size: truncate + warn.

architecture: change detection. Hash entire document text after stripping TODO keywords, priority cookies, tags, property drawers, planning lines (SCHEDULED/DEADLINE/CLOSED), and timestamps. Normalize whitespace. Hash with secure-hash sha256. Stored per file in file_hashes table. On hash change: DELETE all embeddings rows whose node_id belongs to any node in that file, update file_hashes row, then queue all nodes for async regeneration. This is a file-level (not per-heading) hash.

architecture: SQLite backend. Separate org-roam-embeddings.db alongside org-roam.db. Use Emacs 29 built-in sqlite API: sqlite-open, sqlite-execute, sqlite-select. Never use emacsql. Connection singleton in org-roam-semantic--db-connection. sqlite-vec extension: attempt load from org-roam-semantic-sqlite-vec-path on DB open; fall back to TEXT storage if unavailable. Cosine similarity ALWAYS computed in Emacs Lisp regardless of storage format.

architecture: DB schema. Two tables in org-roam-embeddings.db. file_hashes: (file TEXT PRIMARY KEY, content_hash TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL). embeddings: (node_id TEXT NOT NULL, chunk_type TEXT NOT NULL, embedding BLOB NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (node_id, chunk_type)). chunk_type is the string 'leading' or 'full'. node_id is the org :ID: property value (same as org-roam nodes table).

architecture: search functions. Three public interactive functions: org-roam-semantic-find-best (both chunk types, dedup by node-id keeping best cosine score), org-roam-semantic-find-best-full (full chunks only), org-roam-semantic-find-best-leading (leading chunks only). All accept (query-text &optional limit cutoff). Return list of (node-id similarity file-path heading-text). Display *Similar Notes* buffer with clickable [[id:...]] links. Query embedding is always synchronous.
